name: Change Management
description: Production changes are documented, approved, tested, and deployed under the change management process before being released. This control ensures that unauthorised or untested changes cannot reach production and creates an auditable record of who approved what, when, and against which policy.

attributes.text: Every production change has a corresponding change request that documents scope, risk classification, and rollback plan
testable_criteria: Does a change request exist for the production change?
Does the change request have a change request identifier in the CHG-YYYY-NNNN format?
Does the change request document scope, including systems affected, code repositories, and config surfaces?
Does the change request include a risk classification of Low, Medium, or High?
Does the change request include a rollback plan?
Does the change request name the requester?
Does the change request name the approver?
Does the change request include planned start and end times?
Is the change in scope (i.e. NOT a documentation-only change, break-fix hotfix classified as Incident Response, or a change to development/staging environments)?

attributes.text: Production changes are approved by an authorised change approver prior to deployment
testable_criteria: Is the approver a different named human from the requester (four-eyes principle)?
Does the approver hold the Change Approver role in the identity provider?
Did approval occur before the deployment window opened?
IF the change is a Standard change THEN is there at least 1 approver (Change Coordinator)?
IF the change is a Normal change THEN are there at least 2 approvers (Change Coordinator + Change Approver from CAB) and was CAB approval obtained?
IF the change is an Emergency change THEN was it approved by a Duty Change Approver and did a retrospective CAB review occur within 5 business days?
IF approval is retrospective THEN is the change classified as an Emergency change and documented as a retrospective approval?

attributes.text: Pre-deployment testing is completed and evidence retained before release
testable_criteria: IF the change is Standard or Normal THEN is pre-deployment testing evidence (test plan + test results) attached to the change request?
Does the testing include, at minimum, functional smoke tests of the affected surfaces?
IF the change is Standard or Normal AND tests failed THEN was deployment blocked?
IF the change is an Emergency change THEN was post-deployment verification test evidence captured within 24 hours?
IF the change is an Emergency change AND tests failed THEN is the failure documented as accepted risk?
Was the testing evidence retained before release?
